paragraph: Single-family homes in Lodi are moving in a real seller's market, with a median sale price of $626,388 and three quarters of homes selling above asking. The sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.02, and median days on market is a tight 21 days, which tells you that priced homes are finding buyers quickly. Multi-family properties are even stronger, with 23 sales at a median of $835,000 and 78 percent of them closing above list price. Those homes are taking longer to move at a 33-day median, but when they do sell, they're commanding serious money. The condo and townhouse category is too thin to read meaningfully at only 3 sales, though all three sold above asking. If you're shopping in Lodi, understand that single-family and multi-family inventory is moving in a competitive market where sellers have leverage. Pricing your home right matters if you want it to move predictably, and if you're buying, you should expect multiple-offer situations on the properties that hit the market well-positioned.
